'''Lance Koro Hohaia''' (born 1 April 1983), also known by the nickname of "'''The Huntly Hurricane'''",<ref>New Zealand Rugby League, ''The 2009 Kiwis'' (Auckland, Sentra Printing 2009) p.12</ref> is a New Zealand former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. A [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand international]] representative utility back, he played in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and the [[Super League]] for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], with whom he won the [[2014 Super League championship]]. In 2008 Hohaia was a member of the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]] winning [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] team.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Early years==
Hohaia was born in [[Hamilton, New Zealand|Hamilton]] on New Zealand's North Island, but grew up in the town of [[Huntly, New Zealand|Huntly]], where he started to play for Huntly South Rugby League Club at the age of six, in 1989. The following year, he joined [[Taniwharau Rugby League Club]].<ref name=Rex>{{cite journal|url=http://sirpeterleitch.co.nz/couch/uploads/file/newsletters/mb_newsletter-112.pdf|title=Lance Koro Hohaia - the Early Years|first=Rex|last=Hohaia|journal=Sir Peter Leitch's Mad Butcher Club Newsletter|date=10 March 2016|volume=112|page=13|access-date=10 November 2017}}</ref>

Hohaia attended Huntly Primary, and [[Southwell School]] and [[St Paul's Collegiate School]], both in Hamilton.<ref name=Rex/><ref>[http://warriors.sitesuite.ws/plugins/newsfeed.cgi?rm=content&plugin_data_id=25952 World Cup Stars for Vodafone Warriors trial in Hamilton]{{Dead link|date=April 2019 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }} ''Warriors Official Site''</ref> Rugby union was the winter sport at Southwell, and at St Paul's, Hohaia played union for the school on Saturdays and league for Taniwharau on Sundays. He played his first senior game for Taniwharau in 2001, aged 17.<ref name=Rex/> Hohaia represented [[Waikato rugby league team|Waikato]] in 2001 and was also selected for the Northern Districts side that lost to the [[2001 French rugby league tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|touring French side]].<ref>[http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P2-24574230.html Waikato five to play France]{{dead link|date=February 2019|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} ''Waikato Times'', 6 June 2001</ref> After six Premier Division games for Taniwharau, he signed for [[Manurewa Marlins]].<ref name=Rex/>

==Playing career==
===National Rugby League===
Lance was signed by the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and he moved up to Auckland. He made his début in Round 4 2002 against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]]. Hohaia played for the Warriors from the interchange bench in their [[2002 NRL Grand Final]] loss to the [[Sydney Roosters]]. Hohaia also made his international début for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|Kiwis]] in 2002.

Hohaia had great utility value and was often used by the Warriors at [[Rugby league positions#dummy half|dummy half]]. However this utility value worked against Lance, as he struggled to claim a regular starting spot in the Warriors lineup and was often in and out of the team. When not selected by the first grade side he has played for the [[Manurewa Marlins]], [[Waicoa Bay Stallions]] and [[Auckland Vulcans]] in the [[Bartercard Cup]] and [[NSWRL Premier League]].

In 2007 Hohaia spent most of the season at centre, due to injuries to the outside backs. However, in 2007 he was again selected for the Kiwis, this time for their tour of Great Britain. At the end of the season the [[Catalans Dragons]] were rumored to be interested in signing Hohaia to replace the retiring [[Stacey Jones]], however Hohaia ultimately stayed with the Warriors.<ref>[http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/head-left-hanging/2007/10/18/1192300949146.html Head left hanging as Catalans line up swoop for Hohaia] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071020030253/http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/head-left-hanging/2007/10/18/1192300949146.html |date=2007-10-20}} ''League HQ'', 19 October 2007</ref>
 
In 2008, Hohaia took over the fullback role from the injured [[Wade McKinnon]]. In August 2008, Hohaia was named in the [[New Zealand national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|New Zealand training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Kiwis select Sonny Bill">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949034,00.html|title=Kiwis select Sonny Bill|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-08-08|access-date=2008-08-08}}</ref> Hohaia was promising in McKinnon's absence leaving a dispute whether he should be played at fullback for the 2008 preliminary final. In October 2008, he was named in the final 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#New Zealand|Kiwi]] squad.<ref name="Kiwis to wait on Webb and Matai">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7656238.stm|title=Kiwis to wait on Webb and Matai|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-07|access-date=2008-10-07}}</ref> He played at fullback in every one of New Zealand's matches, including their victory in the final.
[[File:Lance Hohaia NewZealand.JPG|thumb|right|Hohaia playing for New Zealand in 2009]]
In 2009, Hohaia was injured in the first game of the season against the Eels, He made his comeback in round 5 against the Roosters where he played fullback in Wade McKinnon's absence. Hohaia was named at fullback in the mid season [[ANZAC Test]] against the Kangaroos. He also was selected for the Kiwis 24 man squad for the Four Nations tournament in Europe. He was selected to play fullback against both Tonga and Australia and won the man of the match award in the test vs Australia. In the Four Nations tournament, he appeared at fullback during most of the games and was one of the Kiwis' best players in the tournament. His efforts earned him a try against Australia and a double against France; one of which was a one hundred metre dash.

In 2010, Hohaia became the first-choice fullback ahead of [[Wade McKinnon]]. He played his 150th first grade game for the club.  For the [[2010 Anzac Test]], Hohaia was selected to play for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at {{rlp|fb}} in their loss against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].

====Farewell====
On 12 April 2011, it was announced that Hohaia had signed a 4-year contract with [[Super League]] club, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] from 2012.<ref>[http://nz.sports.yahoo.com/news/article/-/9178384/hohaia-leaves-warriors-st-helens/ Hohaia leaves Warriors for St Helens] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719161836/http://nz.sports.yahoo.com/news/article/-/9178384/hohaia-leaves-warriors-st-helens/ |date=2011-07-19}} ''nz.sports.yahoo.com'', 12 April 2011</ref> On Sunday 13 November 2011 at a farewell function at the Taniwharau RLFC before departing for a 4 year deal sourced by player manager [[Peter Brown (rugby league)|Peter Brown]], with English Super League club [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] beginning 2012, President Takaroa Raihe bestowed an honorary membership for life, of the club on Lance Hohaia for his contribution to both junior and senior grade football. On Saturday 24 January 2015 as part of the Taniwharau RLFC 70 year anniversary celebrations, Hohaia was named five-eight and the Player of the 1st 70 Years from a club team full with local legends and ex-Kiwis.

===Super League===
Whilst playing for English club St. Helens, Hohaia was selected to play for the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]] against [[England national rugby league team|England]] in the first game of the [[2012 International Origin series]].

St. Helens reached the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]] and Hohaia was selected to play at [[Five-eighth|stand-off]]. He was knocked unconscious by a punch from [[Ben Flower]] in the first few minutes of the match and had to leave the field for the remainder of the 14–6 victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/11156623/St-Helens-14-Wigan-Warriors-6-Moment-of-madness-from-Wales-international-Ben-Flower-costs-Wigan-dear.html|title= St Helens 14 Wigan Warriors 6: Moment of madness from Wales international Ben Flower costs Wigan dear|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/11/st-helens-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title= St Helens win Grand Final after Wigan's Ben Flower is sent off|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Guardian |access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/29559845|title= St Helens 14–6 Wigan Warriors|date= 11 October 2014 |work=BBC Sport|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last1=Cartwright|first1=Phil|title=St Helens v Wigan as it happened|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/live/rugby-league/29416637|access-date=12 October 2014|work=[[BBC Sport]]|publisher=[[BBC]]|date=11 October 2014}}</ref>

===Retirement===
After the punch incident in the St Helens v Wigan match [[Ben Flower]] was sent off however Hohaia suffered from ongoing concussion issues. After eight matches of the following season he was forced to retire from the game on 29 April 2015 because of the recurrent concussion symptoms.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/32515217|title=Lance Hohaia: St Helens half-back announces retirement|date=29 April 2015|website=BBC.co.uk|publisher=[[BBC Sport]]|access-date=29 April 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/32525472|title=Lance Hohaia retires because of 'recurrent concussion symptoms'|last1=Fox|first1=Kieran|date=29 April 2015|website=BBC.co.uk|publisher=[[BBC Sport]]|access-date=1 May 2015}}</ref> 

After retiring he moved to [[Michigan]] with his wife and two sons where he now works as a real estate agent. His concussion issues have reportedly subsided.<ref>stuff.co.nz
[http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/90019434/how-lance-hohaia-reinvented-himself-as-a-real-estate-agent-in-michigan how lance hohaaia reinvented himself].</ref>

==Legacy==
In 2015, he was named as [[Taniwharau Rugby League Club|Taniwharau's]] best player of their first 70 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/65740747/lance-hohaia-named-taniwharau-rugby-league-clubs-best|title=Lance Hohaia named Taniwharau rugby league club's best|author=AARON GOILE|date=3 February 2015|work=[[Stuff (website)|Stuff]] }}</ref>

==Coaching==
On 30 April 2021, it was announced that he had taken the head coach role of new US club [[Austin Armadillos]] in the inaugural season of the [[North American Rugby League]] for 2021.<ref>{{cite web|website=St Helens Reporter|date=30 Apr 2021|access-date=1 May 2021|title=Former Saint Lance Hohaia lands coaching role at Austin Armadillo in US|url=https://www.sthelensreporter.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/former-saint-lance-hohaia-lands-coaching-role-at-austin-armadillo-in-us-3221041}}</ref>
